title: Trifolium altissimum
taxonomy: Trifolium altissimum is classified in the kingdom Plantae, and further classified as follows: it belongs to the family Fabaceae, genus Trifolium, and is a species of flowering plant. The genus Trifolium is commonly known as the clover genus, and is characterized by its trifoliate leaves. The family Fabaceae, also known as the legume family, is a large and diverse group of plants that includes many species of trees, shrubs, and herbs. This classification places Trifolium altissimum within the broader context of flowering plants and legumes. The specific classification of Trifolium altissimum reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Fabaceae family.
uses: Trifolium altissimum is commonly known as Big Trefoil, and it has been found to have potential medicinal uses. Its leaves and flowers contain flavonoids, which have been reported to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Trifolium altissimum may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal plant. Further studies are necessary to fully explore the potential benefits of using Trifolium altissimum for medicinal purposes.

constituents: Trifolium altissimum, also known as the tall red clover, contains a range of active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. These compounds have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. preparations: Trifolium altissimum, also known as big trefoil, is a plant native to Europe and Asia, and its herbal preparations have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. side_effects: Unfortunately, I do not have any information on the side effects of Trifolium altissimum. intro: Trifolium altissimum, commonly known as Big Trefoil or Tall Clover, is a perennial legume species native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its tall stem and clusters of pink or white flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The bioactive constituents of Trifolium altissimum include flavonoids, isoflavones, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Big Trefoil is often used in traditional herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders and menopausal symptoms.
